structure_description: 17-section entry-strategy brief. Front matter (cover/methodology/TOC) -> single dark divider opening the entry-strategy spine -> 2-page exec summary that recommends one entry mode and a year-3 ambition -> compressed market and competitive context (3 pages total) -> regulatory and channel mechanics -> partner shortlist -> capex + opex build -> 3-line P&L scenario chart (centerpiece) -> 5x5 risk matrix -> phased entry plan timeline -> methodology endnote. Differs from market_analysis by collapsing 8+ sizing pages into 3 compressed pages and spending the rest on actionable entry mechanics.

sections.purpose: Report cover - title with 'entry strategy' descriptor, country/industry/year, KIRA branding, report ID, confidential tag
sections.notes: Pure template fill from parsed topic metadata. Title pattern appends 'entry strategy' to make the deliverable type unambiguous on the buyer's desk.

sections.purpose: Two-column overview of research approach: primary sources (operator interviews where available), secondary sources, KIRA scenario triangulation methodology, source-tagging conventions
Table of contents - generated from this structure plus page counter
sections.notes: Generated at assembly time, not content-gen time.

sections.purpose: Single dark-mode chapter break opening the entry-strategy spine. Carries a thesis statement on the entry question - e.g. 'A market worth entering, but only on terms.' Sets register for the rest of the report.
sections.notes: Entry-strategy blueprint uses ONE divider, not five. The decision-document format reads as a continuous argument, not five thematic chapters.

sections.purpose: Two-page executive summary. Page 1 (exec_summary_p1): 4 callouts (addressable opportunity USD, recommended entry mode, year-3 revenue ambition, payback period) + 2-col narrative leading with the recommendation + 1 anchor chart (compressed opportunity sizing). Page 2 (exec_summary_p2_implications): 5-card grid framing the entry decision across the canonical pathways - Build / Partner / Acquire / License / Stay out. One card per pathway, even when one is clearly recommended; the report shows the option set, not just the chosen path.
sections.char_budget_hint: Page 1 callouts: num_max=12, label_max=30 (mono UC), change_line_max=38; narrative_left total_chars_max=1200. Recommended entry mode MUST appear in one callout AND in narrative paragraph 1. Page 2 cards: verb-led headline cap 60, body cap 360, anchor number with source tag. Each pathway card explicitly recommends or rules out the path - no fence-sitting. Recommended pathway carries a visual marker (border accent or checkmark) at render time.

sections.purpose: Compressed market_analysis-style sizing: total market USD bn, historical + forecast CAGR, addressable subset for an entrant (not whole market). Single page only - the entry blueprint assumes sizing is context, not the centerpiece. Chart: addressable opportunity wedge inside total market.
sections.char_budget_hint: If topic genuinely needs deeper sizing, orchestrator should suggest market_analysis blueprint instead. This page caps at 1; do not expand.

sections.title_pattern: Target customer segment: definition and sizing
sections.purpose: Named target customer segment (e.g. urban middle-class households / mid-market manufacturers / mass-affluent retail banking customers / SMB logistics operators). Demographic + firmographic definition, segment size, growth rate, why this segment first. Chart: segment-size-vs-total + growth trajectory.
sections.char_budget_hint: Body must answer three questions: who they are (definition cap 220), how many (sizing number with source tag), why first (1-paragraph rationale cap 600).

sections.purpose: Compressed competitive_landscape: HHI / concentration view + top-5 player mini-grid in one page. Each of the 5 players gets a quarter-card with revenue, share, segment focus, and the one structural advantage to neutralize. No deep profile pages - the entry blueprint cares about WHO TO BEAT, not deep biographies.
sections.skip_if: fewer than 3 named competitors identifiable from research (rare - escalate to orchestrator if so)
sections.char_budget_hint: 5 mini-cards each: player_name cap 30, revenue+share line cap 60, segment_focus cap 60, structural_advantage cap 110. HHI viz + sidebar narrative <= 600 chars total.

sections.purpose: Foreign ownership caps, sector licensing requirements, timeline-to-license (weeks/months), one-off application cost, ongoing compliance burden, recent regulatory shifts that affect entry. Chart: license timeline + cost-stack visualization.
sections.char_budget_hint: Must name the specific regulator (e.g. BKPM for Indonesia FDI, MIC for Vietnam, BOI for Thailand) and the specific license category. Generic 'regulatory environment' framing rejected at QC.

sections.purpose: Channel mix options for an entrant: own retail vs distributor vs e-commerce vs platform vs agent network. Per-channel: setup cost, ramp speed, unit economics, reach ceiling. Chart: 2x2 of cost-to-establish vs reach-ceiling with each channel plotted as a bubble sized by year-3 revenue contribution.
sections.char_budget_hint: Must name 4-5 channel options specific to the country (e.g. Indonesia: traditional trade via Alfamart/Indomaret, modern trade via Hypermart, e-commerce via Tokopedia/Shopee, B2B via local distributors, direct via own boutique). Generic channel labels rejected at QC.

sections.purpose: Named shortlist of 6-9 candidate JV partners / distributors / agents. Each carries: organisation name, ownership group, segment footprint, revenue band, prior foreign-partner track record. Visualised as a grouped scoring matrix (rows = candidates, columns = fit/scale/track-record/ownership-flexibility scores).
sections.skip_if: country + industry combination has too thin an identifiable partner set (rare - orchestrator escalation)
sections.char_budget_hint: Each candidate row: org_name cap 30, ownership_group cap 30, segment_footprint cap 60, revenue_band cap 20 (e.g. 'USD 200-400 mn'), score_strip cap 30. Anchor narrative beneath table <= 600 chars naming the top-2 recommended candidates.

sections.title_pattern: Capex requirements: build versus partner economics
sections.purpose: Capex build: factory / retail rollout / fleet / platform / inventory - whichever applies to this industry. Side-by-side: full-build capex stack vs partner-route capex stack. Chart: stacked bar of capex categories per route + a USD total line above each.
sections.char_budget_hint: Capex categories named to the industry (e.g. 'land + factory build' for manufacturing, 'store fit-out + opening inventory' for retail, 'platform build + integration' for fintech). Generic 'fixed assets' rejected at QC.

sections.purpose: Staffing headcount ramp Y1->Y2->Y3 by function (commercial / ops / tech / G&A), all-in cost per headcount band (local market benchmarks), opex envelope per year. Chart: stacked area showing opex composition over 36 months + a headcount line overlay.
sections.char_budget_hint: Local-market salary benchmarks must carry [secondary] or [estimate] tag with anchor (e.g. country compensation surveys). Sidebar narrative <= 500 chars explaining the ramp shape and key inflection (e.g. 'commercial doubles in Y2 as own-retail footprint expands').

sections.purpose: CENTERPIECE. 3-line chart projecting Y1/Y2/Y3 revenue (or contribution margin if the topic warrants) across base / bull / bear scenarios. Beneath the chart: 3 anchor cards naming the single key driver per scenario (e.g. base = own-channel ramp on plan / bull = early distributor exclusive / bear = regulatory delay + slow license).
sections.char_budget_hint: Scenario y3 spread bull-to-bear >= 1.8x (QC gate). Each anchor card: scenario_name cap 14, year3_value cap 14, key_assumption cap 110, source_tag required. Chart unit must be named in y-axis label (USD mn or USD bn).

sections.purpose: 5x5 likelihood-x-impact heatmap. 8-12 named risks placed across cells, color-coded green/amber/red. Risks must tie back to specific findings from sections 10-14 (regulatory / channel / partner / capex / opex). Right rail: 3-card sidebar expanding the highest-severity risks with mitigation framing.
sections.char_budget_hint: Each placed cell: cell_label cap 22 chars. Sidebar cards: risk_name cap 30 (mono UC), severity_tag cap 14, body cap 180 (framing + mitigation). Risks must include at least one FX/macro risk, one regulatory risk, one operational risk, one competitive-response risk; rest are industry-specific.

sections.purpose: Timeline visualisation of the recommended entry path: 0-12 months (foundation - license, partner, first hires), 13-24 months (commercial launch + first revenue), 25-36 months (scale to year-3 ambition). Reused forecast_outlook schema - the 3 scenario cards become 3 phase cards instead.
sections.char_budget_hint: Each phase card: phase_name cap 22 (e.g. 'Phase 1 - foundation'), milestone_anchor cap 18 (e.g. 'License granted'), body cap 200 listing 3-4 concrete deliverables. Chart slot becomes a horizontal Gantt-style timeline with workstream rows (license / hire / partner / build / launch).

sections.purpose: Source mix breakdown ([primary]/[secondary]/[estimate] percentages), key data anchors, KIRA estimate methodologies disclosed (especially around capex/opex benchmarks and P&L scenarios), contact & next research footer.
sections.notes: Required - decision-document buyers especially value transparent methodology because the P&L scenarios drive their go/no-go call. Auto-generated from source-tag census of the report.

assembly_rules.page_numbering: Continuous, format '012 / NN' in page-header counter slot - total NN computed at assembly
assembly_rules.section_numbering: section_num shared across all pages of a section. The exec_summary entry (id 05) is a single section with page_count 2 (p1 + p2_implications combined), matching the market_analysis convention.
assembly_rules.divider_placement: Single dark divider opens the entry-strategy spine (id 04). No further dividers - the decision-document format reads as one continuous argument, not thematic chapters
assembly_rules.skip_condition_handling: If skip_if condition triggers, omit the section AND recompute page counter for all subsequent sections. For id 08 (competitors) and id 11 (partners), skipping should escalate to orchestrator since absence of competitors/partners signals the topic may not fit entry_strategy at all
assembly_rules.page_type_reuse_note: Id 16 (phasing plan) reuses the forecast_outlook page schema but substitutes 3 phase cards for the 3 scenario cards. The chart_main slot becomes a horizontal Gantt-style timeline instead of a multi-period projection. content_per_section prompt must be told this explicitly.
